message:
Expand terminal capabilities, inline mode, and e2e remote unicode testing infrastructure

Enhanced ftui-core with improved terminal capability detection, refined
inline mode rendering, and updated input parser. Updated the terminal
session and TTY layer for better terminal writer integration. Refined
the ANSI renderer and presenter in ftui-render. Updated the runtime
program loop. Expanded the demo showcase visual effects screen.

Added remote unicode rendering e2e test infrastructure with WebSocket
client, failure injection scenarios, and updated test runner scripts.
Updated doctor_franktentui coverage thresholds and tape recording.
Synced beads tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.7","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): accessibility, focus model, and IME composition robustness","description":"Background
- Accessibility and IME are required for inclusive and global terminal usage.
- Canvas/WebGPU terminals can easily regress if these are bolted on late.

Goal
- Make accessibility and IME first-class, deterministic behaviors in FrankenTermJS.

In Scope
- Composition pipeline for IME preedit/commit flows.
- Focus model and keyboard-only navigation guarantees.
- Screen-reader mirror and live announcement policy.
- Reduced-motion and high-contrast integration.
- Cross-browser E2E accessibility matrix.

Out of Scope
- Minimal checkbox-only a11y claims without test evidence.

Why This Matters
- Necessary for real-world adoption and accessibility obligations.

Acceptance Criteria
- IME and keyboard-only paths function reliably.
- Screen-reader mirror behavior is coherent and bounded.
- Browser matrix tests provide reproducible evidence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:36.160403481Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.419218131Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accessibility","frankenterm","ime","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:36.160403481Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:04.736000514Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-36je8","title":"doctor_franktentui verification v2: zero-fake realism + full observability closure","description":"Scope: doctor_franktentui only.

Goal:
- Reach truly comprehensive verification for unit/integration/e2e with no fake/mock business behavior.
- Establish deterministic, high-fidelity e2e telemetry that supports postmortem without reruns.

Current gaps motivating this epic:
- Coverage baseline is below full (line/branch/function not 100%).
- Integration/e2e harnesses still include fake external tool shims.
- E2E logging is useful but not yet standardized as full event-level JSONL with correlation IDs end-to-end.

Definition of done:
- Real-behavior unit/integration tests cover critical branches and failure semantics.
- External tool behavior is verified with real tools/environment contracts in integration and e2e paths.
- Happy/failure e2e workflows emit validated JSONL with per-step diagnostics and artifact hashes.
- Determinism soak checks catch non-volatile divergence with actionable first-diff pointers.
- CI enforces coverage + realism + observability contracts and publishes complete artifact maps.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-18T05:22:10.058775180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T05:42:13.138937503Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","doctor_franktentui","e2e","no-mock","observability","test"],"comments":[{"id":1255,"issue_id":"bd-36je8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Detailed execution overlay: start with A/A1/A2 to generate factual baseline; then execute B-track (coverage closure + realism refactor) and C-track (JSONL telemetry) in parallel where dependencies allow; D-track converts outcomes into non-bypassable CI gates; E-track performs matrix truth reconciliation and final evidence report.","created_at":"2026-02-18T05:22:35Z"},{"id":1260,"issue_id":"bd-36je8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space optimization pass (2026-02-18):
- Added explicit e2e realism task bd-36je8.3.6 to eliminate fake tool shims in scripts (not just integration tests).
- Added bd-36je8.3.7 for repeat-run determinism/flake diagnostics so reliability is validated, not assumed.
- Rewired dependencies to increase throughput: removed coarse bd-36je8.3 -> bd-36je8.1 blocker and replaced with granular realism gating via bd-36je8.3.6 where needed.
- Elevated artifact-map and replay-triage priorities to P1 to keep postmortem UX first-class.","created_at":"2026-02-18T05:40:03Z"}]}

Deliverables:

 {"id":"bd-36k2","title":"Gradients: Perceptual Sampling (OkLab) + DeltaE Monotonicity","description":"## Goal
Make gradients look visually uniform by interpolating in a perceptual color space (OkLab) and validating monotonic perceptual distance.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Convert RGB -> OkLab, interpolate linearly in OkLab, convert back to RGB.
This reduces banding and makes vertical/diagonal gradients feel smoother at low terminal color depth.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Interpolation in OkLab yields smoother perceived steps than linear RGB.
- DeltaE (perceptual distance) across steps is monotonic within tolerance.
- No additional per-character allocation on hot path.

## Implementation Notes
- Precompute conversion matrices and reuse.
- Clamp out-of-gamut values safely.
- Provide fallback to RGB interpolation when OkLab disabled.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_oklab_roundtrip_identity: convert -> back preserves color within epsilon.
- unit_deltae_monotonic: successive gradient samples have non-decreasing DeltaE.
- unit_out_of_gamut_clamp: no overflow or panic.
- snapshot_compare_rgb_vs_oklab: visual diff snapshots for the same gradient.

## Perf
- Microbench gradient sampling (p50/p95). Must stay within +5 percent vs RGB baseline.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-376","title":"Implement interactive widgets (List, Scrollbar, Input)","description":"PHASE 5 - INTERACTIVE WIDGETS

## Background
Interactive widgets handle user input and state changes. List provides selectable items, Scrollbar visualizes scroll position, Input accepts text entry.

## Technical Requirements

### List Widget
- Items with optional styling per item
- Single/multi selection modes
- Keyboard navigation (up/down/pageup/pagedown/home/end)
- Highlight style for selected item
- Scroll into view on selection change
- Item filtering/search support

### Scrollbar Widget
- Vertical and horizontal orientation
- Track, thumb, and arrow styles
- Thumb size proportional to content ratio
- Click/drag support (if mouse enabled)
- Auto-hide when content fits

### Input Widget
- Single-line text input
- Cursor positioning with blinking
- Text selection (shift+arrow)
- Clipboard operations (copy/paste)
- Placeholder text when empty
- Password mode (masked input)
- Input validation hooks

## Design Rationale
These widgets encapsulate common interaction patterns. State management follows Elm architecture - widgets emit events, parent handles state updates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List with selection modes
- [ ] List keyboard navigation
- [ ] Scrollbar sizing algorithm
- [ ] Scrollbar orientation support
- [ ] Input cursor management
- [ ] Input text selection
- [ ] All widgets implement StatefulWidget trait
- [ ] Event emission for state changes

## StatefulWidget Trait
pub trait StatefulWidget {
    type State;
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, state: &mut Self::State);
}

+        if not isinstance(kind, str):

+import json
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+path = Path(sys.argv[1])
+events = [json.loads(line) for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines() if line.strip()]
+if not events:
+    raise SystemExit("failure-injection JSONL is empty")
+
+failed_asserts = [
+    event for event in events
+    if event.get("type") == "assert" and event.get("status") == "failed"
+]
+if not failed_asserts:
+    raise SystemExit("expected at least one failed assert in failure-injection JSONL")
+if not any(event.get("assertion") == "failure_intentional_missing_marker" for event in failed_asserts):
+    raise SystemExit("intentional missing marker assertion was not recorded as failed")
+
+error_events = [event for event in events if event.get("type") == "error"]
+if not error_events:
+    raise SystemExit("expected error events in failure-injection JSONL")
+if not any("failure_intentional_missing_marker" in event.get("message", "") for event in error_events):
+    raise SystemExit("error events do not include assertion identifier context")
+
+run_end = [event for event in events if event.get("type") == "run_end"]
+if len(run_end) != 1:
+    raise SystemExit(f"expected one run_end event, got {len(run_end)}")
+if run_end[0].get("status") != "failed":
+    raise SystemExit(f"run_end status is not failed: {run_end[0].get('status')}")
+PY

+python3 - "$SUCCESS_RESULT" "$FAILURE_RESULT" "$JSONL_OUT" "$FAIL_JSONL_OUT" "$REPORT_OUT" <<'PY'
+import json
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+success = json.loads(sys.argv[1])
+failure = json.loads(sys.argv[2])
+jsonl_ok = sys.argv[3]
+jsonl_fail = sys.argv[4]
+report = Path(sys.argv[5])
+
+payload = {
+    "suite": "remote_unicode_conformance",
+    "status": "pass",
+    "success_scenario": {
+        "name": success.get("scenario"),
+        "outcome": success.get("outcome"),
+        "frames": success.get("frames"),
+        "ws_in_bytes": success.get("ws_in_bytes"),
+        "ws_out_bytes": success.get("ws_out_bytes"),
+        "assertions_total": success.get("assertions_total"),
+        "assertions_failed": success.get("assertions_failed"),
+        "jsonl": jsonl_ok,
+    },
+    "failure_injection": {
+        "name": failure.get("scenario"),
+        "outcome": failure.get("outcome"),
+        "errors": failure.get("errors", []),
+        "assertions_total": failure.get("assertions_total"),
+        "assertions_failed": failure.get("assertions_failed"),
+        "jsonl": jsonl_fail,
+    },
+}
+report.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+PY
